user_query:
We're launching a niche community for global fintech creators who teach everyday people how to optimize bank accounts, reduce fees, and navigate international transfers. Please read the brief and research in input/community_brief.yaml, input/audience_research.csv, and input/constraints.md, then produce three deliverables:

1) output/plan/community_plan.md — a comprehensive, platform-agnostic community build and growth plan tailored to our brief. Include: community brief and positioning; platform selection rationale; channel and role architecture; onboarding flow (with 0–24h critical window and checklist); a weekly content calendar; engagement loop design; moderation guidelines and decision matrix; a growth flywheel with 8–12 tactics and a tiered referral program; monetization strategy with pricing tiers and value justification; event formats with execution templates and cadence; member lifecycle journey and champion program; health metrics dashboard and health score; scaling milestones; difficult situations playbooks; monthly audit checklist. Add two extra sections specific to our topic: (a) Banking Content Safety & Compliance Policy — add clear disclaimers, what’s allowed vs. not allowed, how to handle fee dispute scripts, interest rate claims, and international wire guidance without giving individualized advice; moderation escalation rules for banking topics; jurisdiction notes. (b) International Member Considerations — time zones, inclusivity for non‑US banking contexts, and content localization priorities. Use clear headings so a reviewer can find each section quickly.

2) output/operations/incident_runbook.md — a monitoring and incident response runbook for community automations/bots (e.g., on Discord or Slack). It must be platform‑agnostic and include: periodic health checks, detection of stale/unresponsive processes, a restart procedure with cooldowns, safeguards against restart loops, a simple locking mechanism to prevent concurrent runs, guidance for handling version mismatch scenarios, cleanup of orphaned processes, and notification/alert steps. Keep it high‑level and vendor‑neutral while being specific enough to execute.

3) output/roadmap/tasks.json — a structured kanban roadmap implementing a 7‑stage workflow with these exact statuses: icebox, backlog, ready, in_progress, review, done, closed. Include at least 12 tasks with hierarchical parent-child relationships for major workstreams and at least two blocking dependencies. The JSON should have a top‑level key "tasks" that is an array of objects with fields: id (integer), title (string), status (one of the 7 statuses), parent_id (integer or null), blockedBy (array of integers, may be empty), blocks (array of integers, may be empty), tags (array of strings, may be empty). Include tasks titled exactly: "Banking Content Safety Policy", "Referral Program", "Weekly Content Calendar", "Moderator Guidelines", "Health Metrics Dashboard", "Premium Tier Design", "Event Series Plan", "Onboarding Flow", "Channel Architecture", and "Champion Program". Ensure at least one parent task with multiple child tasks and at least two tasks that are mutually linked by dependency (one blocks the other).

Please justify the platform selection, growth tactics, and pricing choices based on the brief and research. Keep all files text-only and place them under the specified output/ paths.
